overview: The witest28-4 system is a single-component, stateless web application that serves a "Hello World" HTML page directly from a lightweight Go HTTP server. The server handles root path rendering, proper 404 handling for unknown routes, and a /health endpoint — all within one deployable Docker container on Kubernetes. There are no backend APIs or external dependencies; all logic resides in one service component.
requirements: The root path (GET /) must return HTTP 200 with a valid HTML page displaying a prominent 'Hello World' greeting as readable text.
The HTML page must include a proper DOCTYPE, <html lang='en'>, <head>, <title> (e.g. 'Hello World'), and <body> structure.
All undefined routes must return HTTP 404.
The server must respond within 500ms under normal single-user load.
Total page payload (HTML + inline CSS) must not exceed 500 KB.
The page must be fully renderable without JavaScript.
The page must be responsive and display correctly between 320px and 1920px without horizontal scrollbars.
Text contrast ratio must meet WCAG AA (minimum 4.5:1 against background).
HTTP responses must include Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8.
HTTP responses must not expose sensitive server information (e.g., no Server header revealing software version).
The application must expose a GET /health endpoint returning HTTP 200.
The application must not accept or process any user-supplied input.
The page must load fully within 2 seconds on a ≥10 Mbps connection.

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web
  description: Serves a Hello World HTML page and health check.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the main HTML page with a Hello World greeting.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page returned successfully.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the service is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all for undefined routes
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Route not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Service: hello-world-web ### Overview Implement a minimal HTTP server in **Go (stdlib net/http)** that serves a Hello World HTML page. No external Go dependencies beyond the standard library are needed. ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-web main.go Dockerfile ``` ### Server Details - **Port:** 9090 - **Language/Framework:** Go 1.22+, net/http only ### Routes #### `GET /` - Respond with HTTP 200. - Content-Type header: `text/html; charset=UTF-8`. - Return a complete, valid HTML5 document. - **Do NOT set a `Server` response header** (strip it or never set it — Go's net/http sets `"Go"` by default; wrap the ResponseWriter to suppress it). ##### HTML Page Requirements ```html Hello World

Hello World

``` - The greeting must be an `

` element with text `Hello World`. - No JavaScript in the page. - No external resources (fonts, images, scripts, stylesheets). - Total response size must be well under 500 KB (this page is ~600 bytes). #### `GET /health` - Respond with HTTP 200. - Content-Type: `application/json`. - Body: `{"status":"ok"}`. - Suppress `Server` header here too. #### All other routes - Respond with HTTP 404. - Content-Type: `text/html; charset=UTF-8`. - Return a minimal valid HTML page with text `404 Not Found`. - Suppress `Server` header. ### Suppressing the Server Header Wrap `http.ResponseWriter` with a custom type that intercepts `WriteHeader` and `Header().Set` to delete the `Server` key before writing: ```go type secureWriter struct { http.ResponseWriter } func (sw secureWriter) WriteHeader(code int) { sw.Header().Del("Server") sw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code) } func (sw secureWriter) Write(b []byte) (int, error) { sw.Header().Del("Server") return sw.ResponseWriter.Write(b) } ``` Wrap every handler with middleware that applies `secureWriter`. ### Routing Logic Use a single `http.HandleFunc` on the `DefaultServeMux`: - `/` exact match → serve Hello World HTML. - `/health` exact match → serve JSON health. - Anything else → 404 HTML. Do **not** use a catch-all pattern that also matches `/`; instead check `r.URL.Path` explicitly: ```go http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { sw := secureWriter{w} switch r.URL.Path { case "/": // serve Hello World case "/health": // serve health JSON default: // serve 404 } }) ``` ### Dockerfile ```dockerfile # Stage 1: build FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY main.go . RUN go build -o server main.go # Stage 2: minimal runtime FROM alpine:3.19 WORKDIR /app COPY --from=builder /app/server . EXPOSE 9090 CMD ["./server"] ``` ### Edge Cases - `GET /` with a trailing slash `/` only: serve Hello World (already handled by exact path match). - Any POST/PUT/DELETE to `/`: respond 404 (only GET semantics are defined; simplest approach is to not check method — or optionally return 405 for non-GET on `/`). - The server must not panic on any request; recover from panics if needed and return 500. - Do not log sensitive data; minimal stdout logging of request method + path + status code is acceptable.
